title: Occupational exposure respiratory documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports wheeze, cough, breathlessness, chest tightness, symptom variation, nocturnal or seasonal pattern, triggers, atopy and family history, occupational context, respiratory examination, peak flow, FeNO, spirometry, and objective confirmation.
examination.rationale: Supports occupational-asthma history and objective-test context for occupational respiratory exposure documentation; no decision is generated.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports wheeze, cough, breathlessness, chest tightness, symptom variation, nocturnal or seasonal pattern, triggers, atopy and family history, occupational context, respiratory examination, peak flow, FeNO, spirometry, and objective confirmation.

source.title: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.4.6 — clinical history, examination, objective tests, and occupational asthma

examination.text: Supports cough duration and character, sputum, smoking, medicines, occupation and environment, wheeze, rhinitis, heartburn, functional and mood impact, and face-to-face upper-airway, ear and chest examination.
examination.rationale: Supports occupational and environmental exposure review in chronic cough for occupational respiratory exposure documentation; no decision is generated.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports cough duration and character, sputum, smoking, medicines, occupation and environment, wheeze, rhinitis, heartburn, functional and mood impact, and face-to-face upper-airway, ear and chest examination.

source.title: British Thoracic Society Clinical Statement on chronic cough in adults

medication.text: Supports documenting onset, duration, triggers, rhinorrhoea, obstruction, sneezing, itching, crusting, eye and lower-airway symptoms, sleep, snoring, mouth breathing, family, social, occupational, and medicine context without assigning rhinitis subtype.
medication.rationale: Supports trigger, symptom-pattern and occupational-context review in rhinitis for occupational respiratory exposure documentation; no decision is generated.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports documenting onset, duration, triggers, rhinorrhoea, obstruction, sneezing, itching, crusting, eye and lower-airway symptoms, sleep, snoring, mouth breathing, family, social, occupational, and medicine context without assigning rhinitis subtype.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Job and tasks, agent, route, intensity, duration, protection, symptom timing, coworkers, objective tests, diagnosis, reporting, certification and plan remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for occupational respiratory exposure documentation.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
